message:
fix(pager): update publish_commit tests for monotonic db_size semantics

Commit 49d0b194 made PublishedPagerState::db_size strictly monotonic
across publish_commit (fetch_max rather than store) to eliminate
BusySnapshot "page N > snapshot db_size" errors from cross-process
stale publishes. Two tests still asserted the old shrink-via-commit
behavior:

- test_publish_commit_sweeps_pages_when_db_size_shrinks → renamed
  test_publish_commit_sweeps_pages_but_keeps_db_size_monotonic, flip
  the db_size assertion to the high-water mark (8) and document the
  invariant in the test body.

- test_stale_larger_commit_publish_does_not_reopen_newer_shrunken_snapshot:
  the authoritative shrinker is now publish_truncate_checkpoint, not
  publish_commit. Route the shrink through the truncate path and keep
  the stale-larger publish_commit at the end — its job is still to
  not regress db_size or re-open truncated pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
